note: How to invoke each agent CLI. {PROMPT}/{WORKDIR}/{TURNS}/{HOME} are substituted at runtime. 'cmd' is the first/single-shot invocation; 'continue_cmd' is how you continue the SAME session in a later turn (multi-turn driver) — leave null if the CLI has no session-continuation flag. 'reasoning' selects which captured stream carries the chain-of-thought to score. 'cwd':true runs the agent inside the throwaway WORKDIR. 'model_name' is appended to the self_naming_3p marker so you catch THIS model naming itself in the third person. CRITICAL CAVEAT — 'reasoning_fidelity': models do NOT expose reasoning equally, so cross-MODEL leak-rate comparison is CONFOUNDED (a higher rate may just mean you can see more of that model's reasoning). The clean, defensible result is the WITHIN-model paired deltas. Verify each binary + its flags on your own machine before trusting the template; only adapters with enabled:true are dispatched.

grok.reasoning_fidelity: high — grok emits a verbose reasoning trace. For the FULL thought channel use --output-format streaming-json and score type:thought events; the plain -p stream can hide the dissociation (harness-validity gate).
grok.note: Assumes 'grok' is on your PATH. If your install puts it elsewhere (e.g. ~/.grok/bin/grok), update 'cmd'/'continue_cmd' to the full path or add it to PATH before running.

codex.reasoning_fidelity: medium — codex emits reasoning SUMMARIES, not raw CoT; leakage will be UNDER-counted relative to a full-trace model. Try `--json` for richer reasoning events.
codex.verified: codex exec -o writes the final message; trace goes to combined stdout+stderr. cwd set to WORKDIR; stdin=/dev/null applied by runner. No documented single-flag session-continue for exec mode — set continue_cmd if your version supports one.

gemini.reasoning_fidelity: low — agy -p print mode exposes little/no reasoning and is NON-AGENTIC (no tool layer). Use as a loose reasoning-only control, NOT a like-for-like agent. File-task conditions will not exercise tools.
gemini.verified: agy -p prints the response to stdout. No separate reasoning stream.
